aids.title: MUX Memory Trick
aids.content:
**M**any inputs
**U**sing select lines
**X**actly one output

2^n inputs -> n select lines -> 1 output
"Many to One selector"

aids.title: MUX Size Formula
aids.content:
For 2^n : 1 MUX:
- **n** select lines
- **2^n** data inputs
- **1** output

Example: 8:1 MUX has 3 select lines (2^3 = 8)

aids.title: 2:1 MUX Expression
aids.content:
Y = S'I0 + SI1

Think of it as a switch:
- S=0: Door to I0 opens
- S=1: Door to I1 opens

Only one door open at a time!

aids.title: Function Implementation Rule
aids.content:
**2^n:1 MUX for n-variable function:**
Connect variables to select lines,
Connect minterms (0 or 1) to data inputs.

**2^(n-1):1 MUX for n-variable function:**
Connect (n-1) variables to select,
Connect 0, 1, last variable, or its complement to data.

aids.title: MUX Rhyme
aids.content:
Many inputs, one way out,
Select lines choose, there's no doubt.
Two-to-one or eight-to-one,
Data routing, smoothly done!

aids.title: Building Larger MUX
aids.content:
**TREE** structure:
**T**wo smaller MUX in parallel
**R**oute to final selector
**E**ach level doubles capacity
**E**xtra select line per level
